lede: A look at July 7 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Moussa Diaby and Mohammad Ashraful, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Boris Johnson
events.desc: Boris Johnson announces his resignation as leader of the Conservative Party following days of pressure from the Members of Parliament (MPs) during the July 2022 United Kingdom government crisis.

events.title: Spiritwood Incident
events.desc: A shootout happens in Spiritwood, Canada, killing 2 Royal Canadian Mounted Police and wounding a 3rd officer.

events.title: Yugoslav Wars
events.desc: Yugoslav Wars: The Brioni Agreement ends the ten-day independence war in Slovenia against the rest of the Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia.

events.title: Lebanese Civil War
events.desc: During the Lebanese Civil War, 83 Tiger militants are killed during what will be known as the Safra massacre.

events.title: Howard Hughes
events.desc: Howard Hughes nearly dies when his XF-11 reconnaissance aircraft prototype crashes in a Beverly Hills neighborhood.

events.title: Peel Commission
events.desc: The Peel Commission Report recommends the partition of Palestine, which was the first formal recommendation for partition in the history of Palestine.

events.title: Colombo Town Guard
events.desc: Colombo Town Guard officer Henry Pedris is executed in British Ceylon for allegedly inciting persecution of Muslims.

events.title: North Pacific Fur Seal Convention of 1911
events.desc: The United States, UK, Japan, and Russia sign the North Pacific Fur Seal Convention of 1911 banning open-water seal hunting, the first international treaty to address wildlife preservation issues.

events.title: XYZ Affair
events.desc: As a result of the XYZ Affair, the US Congress rescinds the Treaty of Alliance with France sparking the "Quasi-War".

events.title: Continental Army
events.desc: American forces retreating from Fort Ticonderoga are defeated in the Battle of Hubbardton.

births.name: Tung Chee-hwa
births.line: Hong Kong businessman and politician, 1st Chief Executive of Hong Kong

births.name: Robert A. Heinlein
births.line: American science fiction writer and screenwriter (died 1988)

deaths.text: Kenny Irwin Jr., American race car driver
Cathal Brugha, Irish revolutionary and politician, active in the Easter Rising, Irish War of Independence; first Ceann Comhairle and first President of Dáil Éireann

observances.text: Christian feast day: Job of Manyava (Ukrainian Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Blesseds Józef and Wiktoria Ulma with seven children (Catholic Church)
Independence Day, celebrates the independence of Solomon Islands from the United Kingdom in 1978.
